ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR STRICT LIABILITY, FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY AND DENIED AS TO NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO RESPIRABLE ASBESTOS FIBERS AS A RESULT OF DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITIES. PLAINTIFF'S HEARSAY OBJECTIONS TO THE TWO MEDICAL REPORTS SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT ARE SUSTAINED. EVEN IF CONSIDERED, THE MEDICAL REPORTS DO NOT NEGATE ANY ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR STRICT LIABILITY IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT MANUFACTURE OR SUPPLY ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS AND A CONTRACTOR IS NOT STRICTLY LIABLE FOR DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS UNLESS IT WAS A SELLER OF THOSE PRODUCTS. (MONTE VISTA DEVELOPMENT CORP V. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 226 CAL. APP. 3D 1681) SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT HE CANNOT PROVE THIS CLAIM AND, WHILE HE REPRESENTS THAT THE CLAIM HAS BEEN DISMISSED, NO DISMISSAL HAS BEEN FILED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY IS GRANTED BECAUSE IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSES EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT WAS THE OWNER OF PLAINTIFF'S WORKSITE OR THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS THROUGH THE ACTIVITIES OF A CONTRACTOR HIRED BY DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patricia M. Keady, Esq. of SEDGWICK (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Peter Kiewit Sons, Inc.
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Russell Utterback

RULING:
DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to plaintiff's claims for strict liability, false representation and premises owner/contractor liability and denied as to negligence. Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to respirable asbestos fibers as a result of defendant's activities. Plaintiff's hearsay objections to the two medical reports submitted by defendant are sustained. Even if considered, the medical reports do not negate any element of plaintiff's claims. Summary adjudication on plaintiff's claim for strict liability is granted because plaintiff concedes that defendant did not manufacture or supply asbestos-containing products and a contractor is not strictly liable for defective products unless it was a seller of those products. (Monte Vista Development Corp v. Superior Court (1991) 226 Cal. App. 3d 1681) Summary adjudication on plaintiff's claim for false representation is granted because plaintiff does not dispute that he cannot prove this claim and, while he represents that the claim has been dismissed, no dismissal has been filed. Summary adjudication on plaintiff's claim for premises owner/contractor liability is granted because it is undisputed that plaintiff does not posses evidence that defendant was the owner of plaintiff's worksite or that he was exposed to asbestos through the activities of a contractor hired by defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
